request_summary: Emit GitHub-ready issue drafts for the Valhalla V3 real-time agent pipeline dashboard. One issue per capability slice, grouped in logical delivery order.

items.title: Discovery: confirm state.yaml schema, execution-log.json schema, and stage-to-phase mapping
items.outcome: All data model unknowns are resolved before BC-01 and INT-01 implementation begins.
scope: Enumerate all status values used in state.yaml across existing projects
Confirm execution-log.json schema: field names, nullability, stage name conventions
Determine how design phases vs build phases are identified from stage names
Confirm premiumRequests field: always present, fractional values, null handling
Check if 40-build/ directory convention is used or if artifacts are always single files
acceptance_criteria: Status values confirmed: active, awaiting_review, blocked, completed
execution-log.json schema annotated with field types and nullability
Design/Build phase derivation strategy documented with agent-name-to-phase mapping table
premiumRequests semantics confirmed: number | null, display as dash when null
Artifact convention confirmed: flat numbered files at run root, 40-build/ is obsolete
items.split_rationale: Discovery must complete before any BC-01 or INT-01 work begins.
items.body:
## Context

This discovery task validates the filesystem data model before any implementation work on the API layer (BC-01) or shared types (INT-01) begins.

## Key Findings (from prior discovery run)

- **Status values**: `active`, `awaiting_review`, `blocked`, `completed` only
- **premiumRequests**: Always `null` in all observed data -- type as `number | null`, display as `--` when null
- **ratatoksr.PNG typo**: Confirmed -- rename to `ratatoskr.PNG` in CC-01-C1
- **40-build/ convention**: Obsolete for valhalla-v2 style projects -- read flat numbered files at run root
- **Agent-to-phase mapping**: execution-log uses agent names; state.yaml uses phase names -- a mapping table is required in the API layer
- **V2 reference**: `valhalla-v2/src/` is a fully built React+TS+Vite+Tailwind app and is the primary reference for V3

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Status values documented: `active`, `awaiting_review`, `blocked`, `completed`
- [ ] execution-log.json schema annotated with field types and nullability
- [ ] Design/Build phase derivation strategy documented
- [ ] `premiumRequests` semantics confirmed: `number | null`, treat null as 0 for cost calculations
- [ ] Artifact path convention confirmed: flat numbered `.md` files at run root

## Technical Notes

- All downstream issues (BC-01-C1, INT-01-C1) are blocked on this
- Design team agents: `loki, mimir, baldr, ratatoskr, sleipnir, freya, heimdall, brokk, sindri`
- Build team agents: `thor, ymir, modi, magni, tyr, valkyrie, jormungandr, fenrir, surtr, hel`
- Cross-cutting: `odin, yggdrasil`

items.title: Design system: shared component library (tokens, AgentCard, JobCard, StageChip, CostBar, AgentAvatar)
items.outcome: All views built from consistent, reusable components with the Valhalla dark theme.
scope: Tailwind config: dark theme tokens (colors, spacing, glow effects)
AgentCard: avatar, name, model badge, status pill (idle/working/blocked)
JobCard: title, issue#, project, agent, duration, cost, pulse animation
StageChip: stage name, status indicator
CostBar: progress bar with labels
AgentAvatar: maps agent name to correct image from public/img/agents/
Rename ratatoksr.PNG to ratatoskr.PNG to fix known filename typo
Centralise Framer Motion animation variants in src/lib/motion.ts
acceptance_criteria: AgentAvatar correctly maps all 21 agents to their PNG files with no 404s
ratatoskr.PNG filename typo corrected and all references updated
Dark theme tokens applied consistently across all primitives
Pulsing glow animation works on JobCard via Framer Motion
All components fully typed with TypeScript
assumptions: Avatar files exist in public/img/agents/ -- 22 files confirmed including yggdrasil2.PNG
ratatoksr.PNG typo is the only filename inconsistency
items.split_rationale: Design system must exist before any view work can begin.
items.body:
## Context

The shared design system provides foundational components and Tailwind dark theme tokens used by every view. Must be implemented before view-level work begins. V2 (`valhalla-v2/src/`) contains existing component patterns to reference.

**Known issue:** `public/img/agents/ratatoksr.PNG` has a typo (missing second s). Must be renamed to `ratatoskr.PNG` in this issue to prevent 404s in `AgentAvatar`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AgentAvatar` maps all 21 agent names to correct PNG files with no 404s after renaming
- [ ] `ratatoksr.PNG` renamed to `ratatoskr.PNG` and all code references updated
- [ ] Tailwind dark theme tokens configured in `tailwind.config.ts`
- [ ] Pulsing glow animation on `JobCard` using Framer Motion (variant in `src/lib/motion.ts`)
- [ ] All primitives fully typed: `AgentCard`, `JobCard`, `StageChip`, `CostBar`, `AgentAvatar`

## Technical Notes

- Reference V2 component patterns; V3 evolves rather than rewrites
- Animation variants centralised in `src/lib/motion.ts` -- no inline variant definitions in view components
- No external component library (no shadcn, no MUI) per architecture decision AD-06
- `yggdrasil2.PNG` exists as alternate image -- document intended use or leave for later

items.title: Application shell: routing, navigation, and mobile tab bar
items.outcome: Users can navigate between all 5 views via desktop sidebar and mobile tab bar.
scope: Vite + React 18 + TypeScript project root scaffold
React Router v6 with routes: /, /backlog, /pipeline, /completed, /analytics
Desktop: left sidebar navigation with icons and labels
Mobile: bottom tab bar
Active route highlighted in nav
Page transitions (fade via Framer Motion)
Global error boundary and loading indicator
acceptance_criteria: All 5 routes resolve to correct stub view components
Desktop sidebar highlights active route
Mobile bottom tab bar functional at 375px viewport
Page fade transition smooth on navigation
Global error boundary renders graceful fallback on uncaught errors
Global error boundary renders graceful fallback UI when a view crashes
assumptions: No authentication required -- local-only tool per architecture constraints
items.split_rationale: Shell and navigation are prerequisite to all view work.
items.body:
## Context

The application shell scaffolds the Vite+React+TypeScript project, registers all 5 routes, and provides persistent navigation chrome. Views can be stubs initially and filled in by subsequent issues.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 routes (`/`, `/backlog`, `/pipeline`, `/completed`, `/analytics`) resolve to view components
- [ ] Desktop sidebar highlights active route with icon and label
- [ ] Mobile bottom tab bar renders and is functional at 375px viewport
- [ ] Fade page transition on route change
- [ ] Global error boundary renders user-friendly fallback on uncaught errors

## Technical Notes

- Reference V2 routing and layout at `valhalla-v2/src/`
- React Router v6 `<Outlet>` pattern for nested layout
- Animation variants from `src/lib/motion.ts` (defined in CC-01-C1)
- Use env var `VITE_API_BASE_URL` defaulting to `http://localhost:3001`
- No auth, no guards required for MVP

items.title: API-Frontend contract: shared TypeScript types for all API response shapes
items.outcome: Frontend and backend share a single source of truth for all API response shapes.
scope: src/types/api.ts defining ProjectSummary, RunDetail, RunStageEntry, AgentConfig, LimitsSnapshot, CostSummary
Backend serialises responses to match these types
Frontend normaliser validates/transforms API responses to store types
non_goals: Runtime validation library (manual types sufficient for MVP)
acceptance_criteria: All API response shapes defined in src/types/api.ts
Backend imports from shared file -- no duplicate shape definitions
Frontend store normaliser imports from same shared file
RunStageEntry.premiumRequests typed as number | null
Status typed as active | awaiting_review | blocked | completed
assumptions: Monorepo layout: API server and frontend SPA co-located so src/types/ importable by both
items.split_rationale: Shared types are an integration seam that both backend and frontend depend on.

items.title: Backend: filesystem reader for projects, runs, state.yaml and execution-log.json
items.outcome: Backend can enumerate all projects and runs, reading status and stage data from filesystem.
scope: Recursive scan of ~/odinclaw/portfolio/projects/
Parse state.yaml per project (runs list, status, current_stage)
Parse execution-log.json per run (stages, agent, model, duration, premiumRequests, tokens)
GET /api/projects and GET /api/projects/:id endpoints
Lightweight in-process cache with 10s TTL
Graceful handling of missing or malformed files
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/projects returns all projects with their runs
Each run includes parsed stage data from execution-log.json
Missing or malformed files handled gracefully (skipped with warning, not 500 crash)
Response conforms to shared API types from INT-01-C1
In-process cache prevents hammering disk on every poll tick
In-process cache with 10s TTL prevents filesystem hammering on repeated requests
assumptions: state.yaml and execution-log.json exist at documented paths
premiumRequests is always null currently -- handled as number | null
40-build/ subdirectory is obsolete per DISC-01-C1
items.split_rationale: Filesystem scan is independent of artifact reading (BC-01-C2) and config parsing (BC-01-C3).
items.body:
## Context

The Express API server is the only component with filesystem access. This implements the core data reader that powers all dashboard views. V2 (`valhalla-v2/server/`) contains a working reference implementation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/projects` returns all projects with their runs and parsed stage data
- [ ] `GET /api/projects/:projectId` returns full run history including execution-log.json stages
- [ ] Missing or malformed files skipped with a warning log -- no 500 crashes
- [ ] Responses conform to shared TypeScript types in `src/types/api.ts` (INT-01-C1)
- [ ] In-process cache with 10s TTL prevents filesystem hammering

## Technical Notes

- **Status values** (DISC-01-C1): `active`, `awaiting_review`, `blocked`, `completed` only
- **premiumRequests**: Always `null` -- type as `number | null`, treat as `0` for cost calculations
- **Artifact convention**: Flat numbered `.md` files at run root -- `40-build/` is obsolete
- Agent-to-phase mapping table defined here; server returns `phase: design | build` per stage
- API bound to `localhost` only
- Reference V2 server code for YAML parsing patterns

items.title: Backend: artifact reader endpoint for stage markdown files
items.outcome: Frontend can fetch rendered artifact markdown for any stage of any run.
scope: GET /api/projects/:project/runs/:run/artifacts/:stage endpoint
Read numbered artifact files (00- through 80-) from run directory
Stage-to-filename mapping defined server-side
Return raw markdown string
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns correct artifact content for valid stage names
Returns 404 with clear message if artifact not found
All numbered artifact conventions supported (00-request.md through 80-release.md)
Stage-to-filename mapping is server-side; client passes stage name not filename
assumptions: Artifact files follow NN-name.md naming convention at run root
40-build/ directory convention is obsolete (confirmed in DISC-01-C1)
items.split_rationale: Artifact reading is a separate concern from project/run scanning.
items.body:
## Context

The Completed view overlay (BC-06-C2) and Backlog view (BC-04-C1) need to render artifact markdown. The API resolves the stage name to the correct numbered file and serves raw markdown.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId/artifacts/:stage` returns markdown content
- [ ] Returns structured 404 with clear message if artifact file not found
- [ ] Stage-to-filename mapping defined server-side (client passes `request`, `architecture`, etc.)
- [ ] All conventions supported: `00-request.md`, `10-architecture.md`, `20-spec.md`, `30-tasks.md`, `50-implementation-summary.md`, `60-review.md`, `70-qa.md`, `80-release.md`, `80-memory.md`

## Technical Notes

- `40-build/` is **obsolete** for current projects -- no subdirectory traversal
- Server returns raw markdown string; client renders with `react-markdown`
- Stage name mapping is a simple static object in the API layer

items.title: Backend: agent config reader for model assignments and Design/Build team structure
items.outcome: API can serve agent metadata (model, team membership, role) to frontend.

Derive Design team and Build team membership from config
GET /api/config endpoint returning agents with model, team, derived status
Agent status (idle/working/blocked) derived from active run stage data
non_goals: Frontend encoding of team membership logic
acceptance_criteria: All agents returned with correct model assignments
Agents grouped into design_team and build_team arrays pre-derived server-side
Agent status derived correctly from active run stages
Team membership follows canonical roster from odinclaw.config.yaml
assumptions: odinclaw.config.yaml structure is stable; agents section has model/provider fields
Design team: loki/mimir/baldr/ratatoskr/sleipnir/freya/heimdall/brokk/sindri; Build: thor/ymir/modi/magni/tyr/valkyrie/jormungandr/fenrir/surtr/hel
items.split_rationale: Config parsing is independent of run data scanning.
items.body:
## Context

The Dashboard Home team panels (BC-03-C2) and Pipeline view (BC-05-C1, BC-05-C2) need to know which agents belong to which phase and what model each uses. Derivation happens server-side -- frontend must not encode pipeline topology logic (architecture AD-05).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/config` returns all agents with model, provider, and team assignment
- [ ] `designTeam` and `buildTeam` arrays pre-derived server-side
- [ ] Agent status (`idle` / `working` / `blocked`) derived from current active run stage data
- [ ] Team membership follows canonical roster from `odinclaw.config.yaml`

## Technical Notes

- **Design team**: `loki, mimir, baldr, ratatoskr, sleipnir, freya, heimdall, brokk, sindri`
- **Build team**: `thor, ymir, modi, magni, tyr, valkyrie, jormungandr, fenrir, surtr, hel`
- **Cross-cutting**: `odin, yggdrasil`
- Config read at startup; optionally re-read on cache miss
- `escalation.thresholds` (max_tasks: 20, fix_cycles: 2) may be included in response for UI warning thresholds

items.title: Backend: limits snapshot reader for API/Copilot rate limit data
items.outcome: Frontend can display remaining API limits without live external polling.
scope: Read limits snapshot file (data/limits-snapshot.json) written by a separate process
GET /api/limits endpoint
Schema: claudeRemaining, claudeResetAt, copilotRemaining, copilotResetAt, updatedAt
non_goals: Writing limits data from this endpoint
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/limits returns limits data if snapshot file exists
Returns null/default values with missing:true flag if snapshot absent
Response includes updatedAt timestamp for frontend staleness indicator
No external network calls from this endpoint
assumptions: Limits snapshot is written by a separate process; this API only reads it
Snapshot schema not yet finalised -- stub with known fields and mark as provisional
items.split_rationale: Limits data has no dependency on run data and can be built independently.
items.body:
## Context

The Analytics limits tracker (BC-07-C3) and Dashboard home cost header (BC-03-C3) need current API limit data. This endpoint reads a locally-maintained snapshot file -- no external network calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/limits` returns limits data when snapshot file exists
- [ ] Returns `{ missing: true, updatedAt: null }` with null fields when snapshot is absent (not a 404)
- [ ] Response includes `updatedAt` timestamp for frontend staleness indicator
- [ ] No outbound network calls

## Technical Notes

Expected snapshot schema:
```json
{
  "claudeRemaining": 450,
  "claudeResetAt": "2026-03-27T00:00:00Z",
  "copilotRemaining": 125,
  "copilotResetAt": "2026-04-01T00:00:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-03-26T18:00:00Z"
}
```

- Snapshot file location: `data/limits-snapshot.json` (configurable via env var)
- Return graceful response rather than 404 when missing -- dashboard must degrade gracefully

items.title: Frontend: Zustand store with 10-second polling loop and global state slices
items.outcome: All views share a single reactive data store refreshed every 10 seconds.
scope: Zustand store with slices: projects, agents, limits, ui
10-second polling interval with AbortController cleanup
Loading and error states per slice
Derived selectors: activeJobs, backlogJobs, completedJobs, agentStatus
Stale data indicator when last refresh > 30s ago
Manual refresh trigger
acceptance_criteria: Store refreshes all slices every 10 seconds
All views consume store slices without making their own API calls
Polling stops cleanly on unmount (no memory leaks)
Stale indicator visible when last successful refresh > 30s ago
Manual refresh button triggers immediate re-poll
Manual refresh button triggers immediate poll bypassing the 10s interval
assumptions: 10-second polling interval is acceptable latency (V3 architecture decision)
Single Zustand store with multiple slices (architecture AD-02)
items.split_rationale: State management is a shared concern that all views depend on.
items.body:
## Context

The Zustand store is the single source of truth for all UI state. A single polling interval fires every 10 seconds updating all slices simultaneously so all views reflect the same data snapshot (architecture AD-02). Views must not make their own fetch calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Store has slices: `projectsSlice`, `activeRunSlice`, `completedRunsSlice`, `configSlice`, `limitsSlice`, `uiSlice`
- [ ] Single `setInterval` at 10s; all views share the same poll tick
- [ ] Polling stops cleanly on unmount via `clearInterval` / AbortController
- [ ] `uiSlice` tracks: loading state, error state, `lastRefreshedAt`, manual-refresh trigger
- [ ] Stale indicator shows when `lastRefreshedAt` > 30s ago

## Technical Notes

- Use Zustand `create` with immer middleware for clean slice mutation
- Derived selectors: `active` = active jobs, `awaiting_review` = backlog, `completed` + `blocked` = completed
- WebSocket/SSE upgrade path in v4: replace `setInterval` with SSE subscription without changing slice interfaces

items.title: Home view: active job hero card with real-time stage indicator
items.outcome: User sees the most recently updated active job prominently with its current stage and agent.
scope: HeroCard component: job title, issue number, project
Current stage indicator: stage name, agent avatar, duration
Pulsing glow animation when status is active
Empty state when no active jobs
Link to full job detail
non_goals: Editing jobs from the hero card
acceptance_criteria: Most recently updated active job shown in hero position
Current stage name and active agent avatar clearly visible
Pulsing animation present when status=active
Clear empty state when no active jobs
Hero card usable at 375px mobile viewport
assumptions: Active job determined by most recent run with status=active in store
items.split_rationale: Hero card is the primary home-view component, independent of team panels and cost bar.
items.body:
## Context

The dashboard home page prominently surfaces the most active in-flight job. The hero card is the first thing a user sees and must immediately communicate what the pipeline is doing right now.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Most recently updated `active` run displayed as hero card
- [ ] Current stage name and active agent avatar clearly visible
- [ ] Pulsing glow animation when `status === 'active'` (from `src/lib/motion.ts`)
- [ ] Clear empty state when no runs have `status === 'active'`
- [ ] Functional and readable at 375px viewport width

## Technical Notes

- Consume `activeRunSlice` from Zustand store -- no direct API calls
- Use `AgentAvatar` and `JobCard` from CC-01-C1
- Active job: sort by `lastUpdated` descending, take first with `status === 'active'`
- Stage and agent info from last entry in execution-log stages array

items.title: Home view: Design and Build agent team panels
items.outcome: User can see all agents in both teams with their current status at a glance.
scope: Two-panel layout: Design Team | Build Team
AgentCard per agent: avatar, name, model badge, status pill (idle/working/blocked)
Active job name shown when agent is working
Mobile: panels stacked with horizontal scroll per panel
non_goals: Clicking agent to drill into agent history
acceptance_criteria: Both teams visible side-by-side on desktop
Agent status derived correctly from active run stages
Model badge shows configured model for each agent
Mobile shows panels stacked with horizontal agent scroll at 375px
assumptions: Agent status idle/working/blocked derivable from execution-log active stage data
Team membership pre-derived by backend -- frontend receives designTeam/buildTeam arrays
items.split_rationale: Team panels are independent of the hero card and cost bar.
items.body:
## Context

The agent team panels give a bird's-eye view of the entire Norse agent system. Users should immediately know which agents are idle, working, or blocked without drilling into a specific run.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design Team and Build Team panels side-by-side on desktop
- [ ] Each `AgentCard` shows: avatar, name, model badge, status pill
- [ ] Working agents show the active job name
- [ ] Mobile: panels stacked, agents scroll horizontally within each panel at 375px

## Technical Notes

- Consume `configSlice` (team arrays) and `activeRunSlice` (agent status) from Zustand store
- Team membership from `GET /api/config` -- frontend must not hardcode agent lists (AD-05)
- Status: `working` if agent is current stage agent in any active run; `blocked` if run status is `blocked`; else `idle`
- Use `AgentCard` from CC-01-C1

items.title: Home view: pipeline flow bar and sticky cost/limits header
items.outcome: User sees high-level pipeline throughput and cost/limits at all times.
scope: Pipeline flow bar: Backlog to Design Phase to Build Phase to Complete with job counts
Sticky cost/limits header: premium requests today/week, Claude remaining, Copilot remaining, estimated total cost
Progress bars for limit usage
CostBar component from CC-01-C1
acceptance_criteria: Job counts in each pipeline phase accurate and update on poll
Cost header shows estimated total cost (premiumRequests * $0.10)
Limit progress bars show percentage used vs total
Cost header remains sticky on scroll
assumptions: Premium request cost is $0.10 per request
Limits data from BC-01-C4 snapshot; may be null/stale
items.split_rationale: Flow bar and cost header are distinct from hero card and team panels.
items.body:
## Context

The pipeline flow bar and cost header provide persistent situational awareness summarising pipeline throughput and cost/limits consumption at a glance.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pipeline flow bar shows job counts: Backlog / Design Phase / Build Phase / Completed
- [ ] Counts update on each 10-second poll tick
- [ ] Cost header shows `sum(premiumRequests ?? 0) * $0.10` (shows $0.00 while null)
- [ ] Limit progress bars show % used for Claude and Copilot
- [ ] Cost/limits header is sticky (remains visible on scroll)

## Technical Notes

- `premiumRequests` is always `null` currently -- cost shows $0.00 until pipeline populates this field
- Limit data from `limitsSlice`; show staleness warning if `updatedAt` > 1 hour ago
- Use `CostBar` from CC-01-C1
- Cost rounding: display to 2 decimal places

items.title: Backlog view: scrollable queued jobs list with expandable request markdown
items.outcome: User can browse all queued jobs and read their full request markdown inline.
scope: Filter runs where status is awaiting_review
Card list: title, issue number, date created, project
Click to expand inline rendered markdown (react-markdown) from 00-request.md
Pull-to-refresh or visible refresh button
Empty state when backlog is empty
non_goals: Creating new backlog items from dashboard
acceptance_criteria: All queued jobs listed in reverse-chronological order
Expanding a card fetches and renders 00-request.md as markdown
Collapse works correctly on second click
Refresh button triggers store re-poll
Empty state shown when no jobs are queued
assumptions: Backlog status is awaiting_review in state.yaml
00-request.md artifact exists for all backlog items
items.split_rationale: Backlog view is a self-contained view with a single outcome.
items.body:
## Context

The Backlog view shows all jobs queued and waiting to enter the pipeline. Users need to see what is coming up and read the full request brief for any queued item.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All `awaiting_review` runs listed in reverse-chronological order
- [ ] Expanding triggers `GET /api/.../artifacts/request` and renders markdown inline
- [ ] Collapse on second click
- [ ] Refresh button triggers Zustand store re-poll
- [ ] Clear empty state when no jobs are queued
- [ ] Scrollable at 375px viewport

## Technical Notes

- Consume `projectsSlice` filtered to `status === 'awaiting_review'`
- Lazy fetch artifact markdown on expand (not pre-loaded)
- Render with `react-markdown` and syntax highlighting
- Use `JobCard` from CC-01-C1 as base card

items.title: Pipeline view: Design Phase horizontal stage flow with job cards

Job cards per stage: title, issue#, project, agent avatar, duration, running cost

Actively processing cards have visible pulsing animation

CC-01-C2 dependency in original slice was a typo for CC-02-C1 (app shell)

Stage-to-column mapping requires coordination with BC-01-C3

items.body:
## Context

The Pipeline view is the most complex view in Valhalla V3. This issue covers the Design Phase section -- a horizontally scrollable flow showing all active design-phase runs at their current stage.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design Phase stage columns in order: Architecture / UX Design / System Map / Capability Slicing / Discovery / Issues / Issue Refinement / Planning QA
- [ ] Active design-phase runs appear as job cards in correct stage column
- [ ] Actively processing cards pulse visually (from `src/lib/motion.ts`)
- [ ] Running cost shows `sum(premiumRequests ?? 0) * $0.10`
- [ ] Horizontally scrollable at 375px -- no overflow clipping
- [ ] Empty stage columns show minimal empty state

## Technical Notes

- Consume `activeRunSlice` from Zustand store
- Current stage from last entry in execution-log stages array
- Agent-to-stage display mapping resolved server-side -- store provides `currentStageName`
- Note: original slice listed dependency `CC-01-C2` which does not exist -- assumed to be `CC-02-C1`

items.title: Pipeline view: Build Phase horizontal stage flow with job cards
items.outcome: User can see all active build-phase jobs positioned at their current stage.

Stage order: Foundation, Implementation, Code Review, Adversarial Testing, Integration Testing, Resilience Testing, Release, Recovery
Same job card anatomy as Design Phase
Separate section below Design Phase with clear visual separator

acceptance_criteria: All build-phase active runs appear at their correct current stage
Section is visually distinct from Design Phase with a clear labelled separator
Stage flow horizontally scrollable at 375px
Empty state shown when no active build jobs
assumptions: Build phase: agent name maps to build team members
Job card design reused from BC-05-C1 without modification
items.split_rationale: Build and Design flows are distinct sections that can be developed and reviewed independently.

reasons: Complex layout must integrate with Design Phase section

items.body:
## Context

The Build Phase section of the Pipeline view shows all active build-phase runs in a horizontal stage flow below the Design Phase section. It reuses the same job card design.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Build Phase stage columns in order: Foundation / Implementation / Code Review / Adversarial Testing / Integration Testing / Resilience Testing / Release / Recovery
- [ ] Active build-phase runs as job cards in correct stage column
- [ ] Visually distinct from Design Phase (clear labelled separator)
- [ ] Horizontally scrollable at 375px
- [ ] Clear empty state when no active build jobs

## Technical Notes

- Reuse `JobCard` from CC-01-C1 without modification
- Build phase identification: agent name maps to build team (`thor, ymir, modi, magni, tyr, valkyrie, jormungandr, fenrir, surtr, hel`)
- Phase determination done server-side by BC-01-C3 -- store provides `phase: design | build` per stage

items.title: Pipeline view: animated stage transition system with Framer Motion
items.outcome: Job cards animate smoothly when transitioning between stages on poll refresh.
scope: Framer Motion layoutId for shared card identity across renders
400ms ease-in-out transition when card moves between stage columns
Respects prefers-reduced-motion media query
No layout shift outside pipeline flow on transition
acceptance_criteria: Card slides from old stage column to new stage column on poll-triggered re-render
Transition duration approximately 400ms ease-in-out
Animation disabled when OS prefers-reduced-motion is active
No visual artifacts or layout jump during or after transition
Stage transitions use 300ms ease-out for enter, 200ms ease-in for exit. Pulse glow uses 2s infinite ease-in-out.
assumptions: Framer Motion layoutId approach used per UX spec
Each job card has a stable unique key (run ID) used as layoutId
items.split_rationale: Animations are a distinct concern added on top of a working static pipeline view.
items.body:
## Context

Once the static Pipeline view is working (BC-05-C1, BC-05-C2), this issue layers in Framer Motion animations so job cards visually slide between stage columns on poll-tick stage transitions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Job card slides from old to new stage column on poll-triggered re-render
- [ ] Transition approximately 400ms ease-in-out
- [ ] Animation disabled when `prefers-reduced-motion` is active
- [ ] No layout artifacts or overlap during transition

## Technical Notes

- Use Framer Motion `layoutId` with the run ID as identifier
- Animation variant defined in `src/lib/motion.ts` -- no inline variant definitions
- Test by manually editing `execution-log.json` to simulate a stage change
- `AnimatePresence` required for entry/exit when card enters or leaves a column

items.title: Completed view: jobs list with summary metrics and status badges
items.outcome: User can browse all finished jobs with high-level cost and duration metrics.
scope: Filter runs where status=completed or status=blocked (terminal states)
Card list: title, project, completion date, total duration, total cost, status badge
Sort by completion date descending
non_goals: Detailed stage breakdown in list view (in BC-06-C2 overlay)
acceptance_criteria: All completed and blocked runs listed
Total cost = sum(premiumRequests * $0.10) with null treated as 0
Total duration = sum of all stage durationMs values
Status badge reflects run outcome (completed vs blocked)
List scrollable at 375px
assumptions: Terminal states are completed and blocked (no failed status per DISC-01-C1)
Duration data available in execution-log.json per stage
items.split_rationale: List view and detail overlay are independent deliverables.
items.body:
## Context

The Completed view shows a historical record of all finished pipeline runs. This issue covers the list view with summary metrics; the full stage breakdown is in BC-06-C2.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All `completed` and `blocked` runs listed, sorted by completion date descending
- [ ] Each card: title, project, completion date, total duration, total cost, status badge
- [ ] Total cost: `sum(stages.premiumRequests ?? 0) * $0.10` (shows $0.00 while null)
- [ ] Total duration: sum of all `durationMs`, formatted as `Xm Ys`
- [ ] Status badge: green for `completed`, amber for `blocked`
- [ ] Scrollable at 375px

## Technical Notes

- Consume `completedRunsSlice` from Zustand store
- **No failed status** -- terminal states are `completed` and `blocked` only (DISC-01-C1)
- Clicking a card opens BC-06-C2 overlay -- wire up click handler here

items.title: Analytics view: performance dashboard with agent durations and success rates
items.outcome: User can identify slow agents, failure patterns, and Design vs Build phase timing.
scope: Average stage duration per agent (bar chart)
Average job completion time trend (line/area chart)
Success/failure/retry rates per agent
Design phase vs Build phase duration comparison (side-by-side bars)
non_goals: Individual run drill-down from analytics (use Completed view)
acceptance_criteria: All charts populated from execution-log.json stage data
Design vs Build duration comparison visible as side-by-side bars
Retry rate shown where retries field is present; N/A if absent
All charts functional at 375px mobile
assumptions: execution-log.json may not have a retries field -- show N/A if absent
Duration data (durationMs per stage) sufficient for analysis
items.split_rationale: Performance charts are independent of cost charts and can be built and reviewed separately.

reasons: Multiple chart types with different aggregation strategies
Design vs Build grouping requires correct agent-to-phase mapping

items.body:
## Context

The performance tab surfaces agent efficiency data: which agents take longest, where jobs get stuck, and how Design phase compares to Build phase.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Average stage duration per agent as bar chart (sorted descending)
- [ ] Average job completion time trend as line/area chart
- [ ] Retry rate per agent where `retries` field exists; `N/A` if absent
- [ ] Design Phase vs Build Phase average duration as side-by-side bar comparison
- [ ] Charts responsive at 375px

## Technical Notes

- Data source: `completedRunsSlice` -- aggregate `stages` across all completed runs
- Phase grouping: `configSlice.designTeam` / `configSlice.buildTeam` (server-derived)
- `durationMs` per stage is the primary duration metric
- `retries` field may not exist in current schema -- default to `N/A`

items.title: Analytics view: limits tracker with usage bars and burn rate projection
items.outcome: User can see remaining API limits and whether they will exhaust before the reset date.
scope: Claude API: remaining requests, reset time, % used progress bar
GitHub Copilot: remaining premium requests, reset time, % used progress bar
Burn rate: requests/day from rolling 7-day execution-log history
Projected days-to-limit with on-track / at-risk indicator
Staleness warning if snapshot is > 1 hour old
non_goals: Live external API polling (reads snapshot file from BC-01-C4)
acceptance_criteria: Both Claude and Copilot limits shown with progress bars
Reset time shown in human-readable format
Burn rate projection shows on-track or at-risk with projected exhaustion date
Staleness warning displayed if snapshot updatedAt > 1 hour ago
Graceful empty state if snapshot file is missing
assumptions: Burn rate calculated from rolling 7-day premiumRequests window
Burn rate will be 0 while premiumRequests is always null -- shows on track trivially
items.split_rationale: Limits tracker has distinct data source (BC-01-C4 snapshot) from cost/performance charts.
items.body:
## Context

The limits tracker gives visibility into API consumption headroom and warns before hitting a rate limit. Reads from the locally-maintained snapshot file (BC-01-C4) -- no live external polling.

Note: burn rate will show 0 while `premiumRequests` remains null -- expected for MVP.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Claude API: remaining/total with progress bar
- [ ] Copilot: remaining/total with progress bar
- [ ] Reset time in human-readable format (e.g. resets in 3 days)
- [ ] Burn rate projection: on-track or at-risk with projected exhaustion date
- [ ] Staleness warning if `updatedAt` > 1 hour ago
- [ ] Clear empty state if snapshot file is missing

## Technical Notes

- Consume `limitsSlice` from Zustand store
- Burn rate window: rolling 7 days of `premiumRequests` from `completedRunsSlice`
- Use `CostBar` from CC-01-C1 for progress bars
- At-risk threshold: projected usage exceeds remaining before reset date

items.title: Completed view: detail overlay desktop layout with accordion and artifact rendering
scope: Full-screen overlay container with accessible close button
Two-column desktop layout (Design stages left, Build stages right)
Expandable accordion sections per stage with agent details
Lazy artifact fetch triggered on expand via BC-01-C2
Rendered artifact markdown with react-markdown and syntax highlighting
Summary footer row with cost/duration/token totals
Accessible focus trap and keyboard navigation

items.title: Completed view: detail overlay mobile tab layout variant
scope: Tab interface for mobile (Design | Build tabs)
Single-column layout below tab bar
Same accordion structure as desktop but stacked vertically
Reuse artifact rendering from BC-06-C2a
Responsive tab indicator and navigation
Functional at 375px viewport

items.title: Analytics view: cost time-series chart with granularity toggle
scope: Daily/weekly/monthly premium request consumption time-series chart
Time granularity toggle switching chart view
Data aggregation logic for each granularity (daily, weekly, monthly)
Clear zero/empty state when no cost data
Responsive at 375px mobile
Use recharts or equivalent lightweight charting library

items.title: Analytics view: cost breakdown charts (project, agent, model tier)
scope: Cost per project bar chart
Cost per agent bar chart
Cost per model tier (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) breakdown chart
Data aggregation from stage-level premiumRequests
Sorting and labeling strategy
Clear empty/zero state when no cost data
Responsive at 375px mobile
